name: Frittata Di Zuchine (Zucchini Frittata)
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon fresh thyme, or ½ teaspoon dried thyme
2 tablespoons Italian parsley, chopped, or 2 tablespoons fresh basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler.
2. In a small skillet over medium heat, warm two tablespoons of olive oil. Add onion and thyme (if using) and sauté until the onion wilts, about five minutes.
3. Cut zucchini stems and navels, halve lengthwise, then crosswise into thin slices. Cook with remaining olive oil over medium heat, stirring a few times, until tender but not mushy, 10 to 12 minutes. Add fresh thyme (if using) and basil or parsley just before zucchini is done.
4. Place the mixture in a colander; let cool and drain excess water for about ten minutes.
5. In a bowl, beat eggs lightly with a fork. Add salt, pepper, and cheese. Stir in cooled zucchini and onion.
6. Pour remaining olive oil into a flameproof 12-inch skillet or omelet pan over medium heat. When hot enough to sizzle, pour in egg mixture, distributing zucchini evenly. Reduce heat to low and cook gently until set, about 12 to 15 minutes. Avoid overcooking eggs.
7. Finish under preheated broiler for one to two minutes until top is golden. Loosen edges with a spatula, slide frittata onto serving plate, and serve warm or cold, cut into wedges.
